new_classes.label_en: PDO Still Wine in Containers of 2 Litres or Less with High Actual Alcoholic Strength (CN 22042174)
new_classes.label_de: Stillwein mit g.U. in Behältnissen bis 2 Liter mit hohem tatsächlichen Alkoholgehalt (KN 22042174)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), presented in containers of 2 litres or less, with an actual alcoholic strength exceeding 15 % vol, classified under CN subheading 2204 21 74 (Chapter 22). See additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) PDO status (via hasGeographicOriginQuality → ProtectedDesignationOfOrigin), (2) container volume ≤ 2 L (via maxContainerVolumeL ≤ 2), (3) the specific CN code (22042174 as codeString), and (4) alcoholic strength > 15 % vol (via alcoholByVolumePercent > 15), which is the numeric threshold that discriminates 22042174 from its PDO siblings in the 22042111–22042178 series (based on the CN subheading structure where 22042174 covers actual alcoholic strength > 15 % vol). The legal note references additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, which defines what qualifies as a PDO wine; the content of additional note 6(a) is not reproduced here so its full definitional criteria cannot be axiomatised — this accounts for the reduced coverage score.
